name: Node.js service memory climbs until OOM after 6 hours
description: A Node.js service processes webhook events from a queue. RSS memory grows from 200MB at start to 1.8GB over 6 hours, then OOMs. Restart fixes it temporarily. No obvious leak in code review. The team has tried increasing --max-old-space-size which only delays the OOM.
task: A long-running Node.js 20 service memory-leaks. RSS climbs from 200MB to 1.8GB over 6 hours processing webhook events, then OOMs. `--max-old-space-size` only delays it. Heap snapshot shows no obvious culprit in app code. Walk me through the systematic investigation: (1) what tools to use (heap profiler, clinic.js, node --inspect, etc.), (2) what specifically to look for, (3) how to distinguish heap leak from external (C++ addon / buffers), (4) common Node.js leak patterns that match the symptoms, (5) how to verify the fix. Don't just list 'things to check' — walk through a decision tree.
expected_outcomes: Distinguishes V8 heap from RSS (external memory)
Covers classic Node leaks: event listeners, timers, closures, async_hooks
Addresses Buffer or ArrayBuffer retention outside V8
Verification that the fix actually worked
